# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React/TypeScript frontend application built with Vite, featuring a comprehensive HR management system with Supabase as the backend. The application follows a component-based architecture with role-based access control and real-time capabilities.

## Key Architectural Decisions

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework Choice**: React 18 with TypeScript for type safety and modern React features
- **Build System**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Component Library**: shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives for accessible, customizable components
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens and dark mode support
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, Context API for global auth state
- **Routing**: React Router DOM with protected routes and role-based navigation

### Backend Integration
- **Database**: Supabase PostgreSQL with real-time subscriptions
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with email/password and magic link support
- **File Storage**: Supabase Storage for document uploads
- **Server Functions**: Supabase Edge Functions for complex business logic
- **Email**: Supabase email service for automated notifications

### Data Flow Patterns
- **API Layer**: Direct Supabase client usage with React Query for caching
- **Real-time Updates**: Supabase subscriptions for live data synchronization
- **Form Handling**: React Hook Form with Zod schemas for validation
- **Error Handling**: Centralized error boundaries and toast notifications

## Core Business Logic

### Authentication & Authorization
- Multi-tenant architecture with company-based data isolation
- Role-based access control with granular permissions
- Secure password reset and employee invitation flows
- Session management with automatic token refresh

### Employee Management
- Complete employee lifecycle management
- Profile management with avatar uploads
- Department and role assignments
- Employment status tracking (active, inactive, terminated)

### Performance Management
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Formal performance reviews with ratings and recommendations
- **Daily Evaluations**: Continuous feedback system with peer reviews
- **Timeline Views**: Historical evaluation tracking with filtering
- **Role-based Visibility**: Different access levels for managers vs employees

### Leave Management
- Automated leave request workflows
- Balance tracking with accrual calculations
- Approval hierarchies based on organizational structure
- Integration with payroll for leave deductions

### Payroll Processing
- Automated payroll generation based on attendance and salary data
- Payslip generation and distribution
- Tax calculations and deductions
- Integration with leave and overtime tracking

### Financial Management
- Invoice generation and tracking
- Client billing and payment management
- Expense tracking and reporting
- Financial dashboard with key metrics

## Database Schema Highlights

### Core Tables
- `companies` - Multi-tenant company data
- `employees` - Employee profiles and employment details
- `departments` - Organizational structure
- `roles` - Permission-based role definitions
- `evaluations` - Quarterly performance reviews
- `daily_evaluations` - Daily feedback entries
- `leave_requests` - Leave management system
- `payroll` - Payroll processing records
- `invoices` - Client billing system
- `projects` - Project management
- `clients` - Client relationship management

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies and departments
- Evaluations link employees with evaluators
- Leave requests have approval workflows
- Payroll integrates with attendance and leave data
- Projects connect employees and clients

## Component Architecture

### Layout Components
- `AppLayout` - Main application shell with sidebar navigation
- `AppSidebar` - Role-based navigation menu
- `TopBar` - Global actions and user menu
- `NotificationBell` - Real-time notification system

### Feature Components
- **Employee Components**: Profile views, forms, selectors
- **Evaluation Components**: Timeline views, rating systems
- **Leave Components**: Request forms, balance displays
- **Settings Components**: Configuration panels for HR settings

### UI Component Library
- Comprehensive shadcn/ui implementation
- Custom theme with Beudox branding
- Accessible components with keyboard navigation
- Responsive design with mobile support

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx` - Application bootstrap
- `src/App.tsx` - Main routing and providers
- `src/pages/Index.tsx` - Legacy entry point

### Core Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts` - Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts` - Permission checking utilities
- `src/lib/utils.ts` - Common utility functions
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` - Database client configuration

### Business Logic
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts` - Leave calculation functions
- `src/lib/notifications.ts` - Notification system utilities
- `src/lib/format-role.ts` - Role formatting helpers

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts` - Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts` - Styling configuration
- `package.json` - Dependencies and scripts
- `supabase/config.toml` - Backend configuration

## Data Flow and State Management

### Client-Side State
- **Authentication State**: User session, profile, and permissions
- **Form State**: Local form data with validation
- **UI State**: Modal states, loading indicators, navigation

### Server State
- **Employee Data**: Profiles, evaluations, leave balances
- **Business Data**: Projects, clients, invoices, payroll
- **Configuration**: Company settings, departments, roles

### Real-time Updates
- Live notifications for approvals and updates
- Real-time evaluation submissions
- Live chat or messaging (if implemented)

## Security Considerations

### Authentication
- Secure token-based authentication
- Password complexity requirements
- Account lockout protection
- Secure password reset flows

### Authorization
- Row-level security in Supabase
- API endpoint protection
- File upload restrictions
- Data isolation by company

### Data Protection
- Input validation and sanitization
- SQL injection prevention
- XSS protection
- Secure file handling

## Performance Optimizations

### Frontend
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Image optimization and lazy loading
- React Query caching strategies
- Virtual scrolling for large lists

### Backend
- Database indexing on frequently queried columns
- Efficient queries with proper joins
- Caching strategies for static data
- CDN for static assets

## Development Workflow

### Code Quality
- TypeScript for type safety
- ESLint for code linting
- Prettier for code formatting
- Husky for git hooks

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for utilities and hooks
- Integration tests for components
- E2E tests with Playwright
- API testing for Supabase functions

### Deployment
- Automated builds with CI/CD
- Environment-specific configurations
- Database migrations on deploy
- Rollback strategies for failed deployments

## Integration Points

### External Services
- **Email Service**: Supabase email for notifications
- **File Storage**: Supabase storage for documents
- **Authentication**: Supabase auth for user management
- **Real-time**: Supabase realtime for live updates

### Third-party Libraries
- **UI Components**: Radix UI primitives
- **Charts**: Recharts for data visualization
- **Forms**: React Hook Form for form management
- **Rich Text**: Tiptap for document editing
- **Icons**: Lucide React icon library

## Future Considerations

### Scalability
- Database optimization for growing datasets
- Caching strategies for performance
- Microservices architecture for complex features
- CDN integration for global distribution

### Features
- Advanced reporting and analytics
- Mobile application development
- API for third-party integrations
- Workflow automation and AI features
- Advanced permission management

This technical context provides a comprehensive overview of the Beudox HR system's architecture, design decisions, and implementation details to help developers understand and contribute to the codebase effectively.
